cards.front: What is the weightage of the Final Exam in the Computer Vision course?
What is the weightage of the Midterm Exam?
How many marks is a student awarded for attendance above 95%?
What is the penalty for copying an assignment from a classmate?
cards.back: All involved students receive zero marks.

cards.front: What is the instructor Dr. Mohammad Asif Khan's PhD specialization?
Which three software tools are primarily used in this course?
cards.back: Python, TensorFlow / PyTorch, and Google Colab.

cards.front: How long is the lecture duration for the course?
What is the consequence of a late assignment submission?
cards.back: The student will receive zero marks.

cards.front: According to the Class Discipline Guidelines, what is the policy for mobile phones during class?
cards.back: They must be kept on silent with no texting or calling allowed.

cards.front: In computer vision, what is an image defined as?
cards.back: A visual representation of a person, object, or scene.

cards.front: The geometric transformation from a 3D world to a 2D image is called _____.
In the perspective projection model, objects appear smaller as they move farther away due to the variable _____.
What does the Latin root *perspicere* mean in the context of perspective?
In perspective projection, all lines used for mapping 3D points to a 2D plane converge at the _____.
Which historical art period first derived the techniques for depicting realistic depth on flat surfaces?
Camera Calibration is the process of determining which two types of parameters?
Which camera parameters describe how the camera maps the 3D world to its internal image plane?
Which camera parameters describe the position and orientation of the camera relative to the world coordinate frame?
The distance between the center of projection and the image plane is defined as the _____.
In Isaac Newton's experiments, what happens when a component color from a prism is passed through a second prism?
cards.back: It does not split into further components.

cards.front: A grayscale image pixel represents only _____.
In the grayscale color space, what pixel value represents pure black?
In the grayscale color space, what pixel value represents pure white?
Which color space is typically used when color is not essential to reduce data and simplify processing?
What is the primary function of a kernel in image filtering?
cards.back: To modify or enhance an image by manipulating pixel values based on a mathematical rule.

cards.front: In Canny edge detection, how are edges classified if their intensity gradient falls between the minVal and maxVal thresholds?
cards.back: They are considered edges only if they are connected to 'sure-edge' pixels.

cards.front: Which category of image features describes the image as a whole and captures overall statistics?
Which category of image features is detected at specific keypoints or local regions?
What is a 'Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix' (GLCM) used to measure?
cards.back: Texture patterns based on how often combinations of gray levels occur next to each other.

cards.front: In computer vision, what is a 'blob'?
cards.back: A region in an image where properties like intensity or color are uniform.

cards.front: How do local features differ from global features regarding spatial information?
cards.back: Local features preserve location ($(x, y)$ coordinates), while global features usually lose spatial arrangement.

cards.front: Which type of feature is most robust to occlusion, scale, and rotation?
Term: SIFT
cards.back: Definition: Scale-Invariant Feature Transform; a local feature extraction algorithm that is scale and rotation invariant.

cards.front: Term: FAST
cards.back: Definition: Features from Accelerated Segment Test; a high-speed corner detection algorithm.

cards.front: What is the primary goal of Feature Extraction?
cards.back: To transform raw pixel data into a reduced set of measurable, meaningful patterns for easier analysis.

cards.front: Which algorithm is described as an efficient, free alternative to SIFT/SURF suitable for real-time applications?
In the SIFT algorithm, how are initial candidate keypoints refined for sub-pixel accuracy?
How many pixels are typically in the circle used by the FAST algorithm to test for a corner?
For a pixel $p$ to be considered a corner in FAST, it must have a contiguous arc of at least _____ pixels brighter or darker than the threshold.
What type of descriptor is used by SIFT and SURF?
What type of descriptor is used by the BRIEF algorithm?
What is the main advantage of the BRIEF algorithm?
cards.back: High speed and efficiency in computation and matching.

cards.front: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) were originally inspired by the study of the brain's _____.
What was the first CNN architecture to stack convolutional layers directly on top of each other?
What dropout rate did the authors of AlexNet apply to layers F8 and F9 to reduce overfitting?
In AlexNet, which two data augmentation techniques were used to reduce overfitting?
cards.back: Randomly shifting/flipping images and changing lighting conditions.

cards.front: A Convolutional Neural Network used for image classification is typically composed of which two main parts?
cards.back: A convolutional base and a densely connected classifier.

cards.front: What components make up the 'convolutional base' of a Convnet?
cards.back: A series of pooling and convolution layers.

cards.front: In the context of pre-trained models, what does 'feature extraction' consist of?
cards.back: Taking the convolutional base of a trained network and training a new classifier on top of its output.

cards.front: Which classic CNN architecture is mentioned as being similar to AlexNet but much smaller?
What is the purpose of the 'max pooling' operation in a CNN?
cards.back: To reduce the spatial dimensions (width and height) of the input data.

cards.front: In Assignment 02, what task must be performed on color spaces like RGB, HSV, and Lab?
cards.back: Show each channel separately and show the image with the brightness channel removed.

cards.front: What are the four compass directions (angles) used when measuring a Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM)?
Which feature detection algorithm uses simple intensity comparisons at pairs of points to form a binary string?
The process of determining camera intrinsics and extrinsics is called camera _____.
What does the 'S' stand for in the SURF algorithm?
Which specific pixels (clock positions) does FAST pick first for its accelerated corner test?
